subject: [PATCH v2 11/14] fuse: add pinned headers capability for io-uring buffer rings

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer Bernd Schubert suggested using the existing io_pin_pages() function instead of duplicating its functionality in this patch, and mentioned that a similar technique could be applied to another project (ublk) as well.
reviews.raw_body:
I think this is a duplicate of io_pin_pages(), can we just export that
and use here? I'm basically going to propose to use the same technique
in ublk - would be another duplicate.


(Not a complete a review yet, just something I just noticed).


Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author agrees that the logic for pinning memory should be moved to the mm layer, not in an exported io-uring function, and asks for clarification on whether the user-allocated memory is related to io-uring.
reviews.raw_body:
Tbh I think this is generic logic that makes more sense to live in the
mm layer instead of fuse calling this as an exported io-uring
function. The memory it's passing in is not related to io-uring, so
that was my hesitation. For your ublk use case, is the memory you're
passing into this user-allocated memory that's not part of io-uring?
If so, then maybe it's best to move io_pin_pages() out of io-uring and
into generic mm.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-02.patch_summary: This patch adds the capability to pin header buffers for io-uring buffer rings in fuse servers. When enabled, the kernel pins the header pages and uses direct memcpy for copying, avoiding per-request overhead of pinning and unpinning user pages. The pinned pages are accounted against RLIMIT_MEMLOCK and tracked using mm->pinned_vm. Unpinning is done during connection abort in process context.
